{{short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Gambia women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=June 2021}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name              = Gambia
| Badge             = 
| Nickname          = The Scorpions
| Association       = [[Gambia Football Federation]] (GFF)
| Sub-confederation = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Confederation     = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach             = [[Johnny McKinstry]]
| Captain           = [[Omar Colley]]
| Most caps         = [[Omar Colley]] (62)
| Top scorer        = [[Musa Barrow]] (14)
| Home Stadium      = [[Independence Stadium (Bakau)|Independence Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = GAM
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|GAM}}
| FIFA max          = 65
| FIFA max date     = June 2009
| FIFA min          = 179
| FIFA min date     = March 2017
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Gambia}}
| Elo max           = 93
| Elo max date      = January 1984
| Elo min           = 145
| Elo min date      = 22 November 1993
| pattern_la1           = _gam23h
| pattern_b1            = _gam23h
| pattern_ra1           = _gam23h
| pattern_sh1           = _gam23h
| pattern_so1           =
| rightarm1             = FF0000
| shorts1               = 0000ff
| socks1                = 22B14C
| pattern_la2           = _gam23a
| pattern_b2            = _gam23a
| pattern_ra2           = _gam23a
| pattern_sh2           = _gam23a
| pattern_so2           = _redtop
| shorts2               = FFFFFF
| socks2                = FFFFFF
| First game         = {{nowrap|{{fb|GAM|colonial|name=British Gambia}} 2–1 [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] {{flagicon image|Flag of Sierra Leone 1916-1961.gif}}<br>([[Gambia]]; 9 February 1953)}}
| Largest win        = {{fb|SEY}} 0–7 {{fb-rt|GAM}}<br>([[Saint-Pierre, Mauritius|Saint-Pierre]], [[Mauritius]]; 14 October 2025)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|GUI}} 8–0 {{fb-rt|GAM}}<br>([[Guinea]]; 14 May 1972)
| World cup apps     =
| World cup first    =
| World cup best     =
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 2
| Regional cup first = [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
| Regional cup best  = Quarter-finals ([[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]])
}}

The '''Gambia national football team''' (recognized as '''The Gambia''' by [[FIFA]])<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Gambia |url=https://www.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/GAM |access-date=4 August 2023 |website=[[FIFA]]}}</ref> represents [[The Gambia]] in men's international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Gambia Football Federation]]. Until 1965, the team and the country were known as '''British Gambia'''. The team represents both [[FIFA]] and [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).{{Citation needed|date=November 2025}}

==History==
Under the name British Gambia, the team played their first ever game on 9 February 1953 against [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]], winning 2–1 at home in a [[Exhibition game|friendly]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.eloratings.net/Gambia |title=World Football Elo Ratings: Gambia |publisher=World Football Elo Rankings |access-date=19 September 2019 |archive-date=18 November 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181118205810/http://www.eloratings.net/Gambia |url-status=live }}</ref> In April 1963, the team entered the ''L'Amitié'' tournament in Senegal, a competition mainly for French-speaking nations. They were drawn in a group with France's amateur team, the Upper Volta (now [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]]) and [[Gabon national football team|Gabon]]. Their first match was lost 5–1 to the French amateurs on 11 April. The Gambia drew 2–2 with the Upper Volta on 13 April, and had the same result the very next day versus Gabon. The Gambia did not advance to the next round.

After the tournament in Senegal, the Gambia did not play another match until 16 November 1968, when they travelled to Sierra Leone to play [[Sierra Leone national football team|its team]] in a friendly and lost 2–1. They played again in Sierra Leone in The Gambia's next match on 24 April 1971, and the hosts won 3–1. On 2 May 1971, The Gambia travelled to Guinea for a friendly and lost 4–2. On 14 May 1972, The Gambia returned to Guinea for their first [[1973 All-Africa Games|African Games]] qualifier and lost 8–0 and were knocked out.

In 1975, the Gambia entered its first qualification campaign for the [[football at the 1976 Summer Olympics|1976 Summer Olympics]] in [[Canada]]. They were drawn in a qualifier against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]], and lost the first leg 1–0 at home on 27 April 1975. The second leg was lost 6–0 in Guinea on 1 June as Guinea advanced 7–0 on aggregate.

In August of that same year, the Gambia entered their first qualification for the [[Africa Cup of Nations|African Cup of Nations]], with the aim of reaching the [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976 finals in Ethiopia]]. They were drawn in a two-legged qualifier against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] and lost the first leg 3–0 away on 10 August. They lost by the same score in their home leg on 24 August and Morocco advanced 6–0 on aggregate.

After the qualification campaign for the 1976 finals, The Gambia played their first match against a full European side, losing a home friendly 4–1 to [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]] on 30 January 1977.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gamb-intres.html |title=Gambia&nbsp;– List of International Matches |publisher=Rsssf.com |access-date=2012-04-20 |archive-date=9 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230309120944/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesg/gamb-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

On 13 October 2002, the team got their biggest ever victory in international competition, a 6–0 victory against [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]].<ref name=11v11>{{Cite web|title=Gambia v Lesotho, 13 October 2002|url=https://www.11v11.com/matches/gambia-v-lesotho-13-october-2002-250388/|website=11v11.com|access-date=11 September 2020|archive-date=5 February 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220205154723/https://www.11v11.com/matches/gambia-v-lesotho-13-october-2002-250388/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In May 2014, the Gambia was banned from all [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] competitions for two years after deliberately falsifying players' ages.<ref>{{cite news|title=The Gambia disqualified from all Caf competitions|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/27268626|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=27 May 2014|archive-date=6 May 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140506061907/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/football/27268626|url-status=live}}</ref>

The nation's fortunes improved in [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualification]] for the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations]]. Although they failed to qualify, they took it to the final round of games, including drawing twice with African giants [[Algeria national football team|Algeria]].

On 13 November, in their first Group D match of [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]], Gambia defeated [[Angola national football team|Angola]] 1–3 in [[Luanda]]. This was the Scorpions' first away win in an [[Africa Cup of Nations|AFCON]] or [[FIFA World Cup]] qualifier ever, at their 40th attempt. An impressive campaign saw them qualify for their first ever major tournament that year. As the lowest-ranked team in the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations]], the Gambia defied the odds by defeating favourites [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] in the group stage, finishing unbeaten in 2nd place in the group. A further win against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] in the Round of 16 saw them reach the quarter-finals at their first attempt, eventually going out to tournament hosts [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]].

==Home stadium==

The [[Independence Stadium (Bakau)|Independence Stadium]] is a [[multi-purpose stadium]] in [[Bakau]], [[Gambia]]. It is currently used mostly for [[association football|football]] matches, although it is also used for music concerts, political events, trade fairs and national celebrations. The stadium holds 30,000 people.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.gambiafa.com/the-gfa/gambia-national-stadium.html |title=Gambia National Stadium |access-date=2013-08-27 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130923092048/http://gambiafa.com/the-gfa/gambia-national-stadium.html |archive-date=2013-09-23 }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Gambia national football team results (2020–present)}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
| date       = 6 June
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|+1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
| score      = 2–1
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|EQG}}
| goals1     = [[Adama Sidibeh|Sidibeh]] {{Goal|7||45+1}}
| goals2     = {{Goal|45+4}} [[Álex Masogo|Masogo]]
| stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]]
| location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
| date       = 9 June
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|UGA}}
| score      = 1–1 
| report     = https://www.sofascore.com/football/match/gambia-uganda/BUbsmVb
| team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Rogers Mato|Mato]] {{goal|27}}
| goals2     =
*[[Alasana Manneh|Manneh]] {{goal|45+1|pen.}}
| stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]]
| location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = D
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|KEN}}
| score      = 1–3
| report     = https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2025/09/05/africa/wc-qualifying-africa/kenya/gambia/4236924/lineups/
| team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
| goals1     =
*[[Ryan Ogam|Ogam]] {{goal|81}}
|goals2     =
*[[Sheriff Sinyan|Sinyan]] {{goal|12}}
*[[Yankuba Minteh|Minteh]] {{goal|26}}
*[[Musa Barrow|Barrow]] {{goal|38}}
| stadium    = [[Moi International Sports Centre|Moi International Centre]]
| location   = [[Nairobi]], Kenya
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Amin Omar]] ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 9 September
| time       = {{UTZ|22:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
| score      = 2–0
| report     = https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2025/09/09/africa/wc-qualifying-africa/gambia/burundi/4236929/
| team2      = {{fb|BDI}}
| goals1     =
*[[Alieu Fadera|Fadera]] {{goal|34|pen.}}
*[[Ebou Adams|Adams]] {{goal|76}}
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Nyayo National Stadium]]
| location   = [[Nairobi]], Kenya
| attendance = 
| referee    = Mohamed Maarouf Eid Mansour ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 10 October
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
| score      = 3–4
| report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/gabon-YJL8W78U/gambia-hADVg0uE/summary/lineups/?mid=fmZL4BGu
| team2      = {{fb|GAB}}
| goals1     =
*[[Yankuba Minteh|Minteh]] {{goal|23}}
*[[Adama Sidibeh|Sidibeh]] {{goal|45+2||47}}
|goals2     =
*[[Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang|Aubameyang]] {{goal|20||42||62||76}}
| stadium    = [[Moi International Sports Centre]]
| location   = [[Nairobi]], Kenya
| attendance = 
| referee    = Joseph Ogabor ([[Nigeria Football Federation|Nigeria]])
| result     = L
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
|format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group F|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 14 October
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|4}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SEY}}
| score      = 0–7
| report     = https://www.soccerway.com/match/gambia-hADVg0uE/seychelles-v5CBuNe8/?mid=t4yY190b
| team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Adama Sidibeh|Sidibeh]] {{goal|2||46}}
*[[Abdoulie Manneh|Manneh]] {{goal|24||67||75}}
*[[Musa Barrow|Barrow]] {{goal|47||52}}
| stadium    = [[Côte d'Or National Sports Complex]]
| location   = [[Saint Pierre, Mauritius|Saint Pierre]], Mauritius
|attendance = 300
|referee    = Washington N'Dolo ([[Liberia Football Association|Liberia]])
| result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|date= 18 November
|time=
|round= [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score= 2–2
|report= https://www.sofascore.com/football/match/kuwait-gambia/mVbsthc
|team1= {{fb-rt|KUW}}
|goals1= *[[Yousef Nasser|Nasser]] {{goal|65|pen.}}
*[[Mohammad Talal Al-Sharifi|Al-Sharifi]] {{goal|90+2}}
|team2= {{fb|GAM}}
|goals2= *[[Abdoulie Ceesay|A. Ceesay]] {{goal|42||57}}
|stadium= [[Al Salam Stadium (Egypt)|Al Salam Stadium]]
|location= [[Cairo]], Egypt
|attendance=
|referee= Mahmoud El Banna ([[Egyptian Football Association|Egypt]])
|format= 1
|result= D
}}

===2026===
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date       = 31 March
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|+0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SEN}}
|score      = 3–1
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/senegal-vs-gambia/4103846
|goals1     = 
*[[Abdoulaye Seck (footballer, born 1992)|Seck]] {{goal|45+4}}
*[[Ibrahim Mbaye|Mbaye]] {{goal|47}}
*[[Lamine Camara|Camara]] {{goal|90+5}}
|goals2     = 
*[[Omar Colley|O. Colley]] {{goal|51}}
|location   = [[Dakar]], Senegal
|stadium    = [[Diamniadio Olympic Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Abdelaziz Bouh ([[Football Federation of the Islamic Republic of Mauritania|Mauritania]])
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date       = 29 May
|time       = 14:00
|team1      = {{fb-rt|IRN}}
|score      = 3–1
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/cesdwwnxbc5fmajgroc0hqzy2/dezv8l0fzgcxtejl0dwmy1gyc/2m1wojm5bt709wu4kugtytxqs/4lic6qsdto0pi15b9l7a65hjo
|goals1     = *[[Aria Yousefi|Yousefi]] {{Goal|47}}
*[[Ramin Rezaeian|Rezaeian]] {{Goal|59}}
*[[Mehdi Taremi|Taremi]] {{Goal|68}}
|goals2     = *[[Omar Colley|O. Colley]] {{goal|42}}
|location   = [[Antalya]], Turkey
|stadium    = [[Mardan Sports Complex]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = {{ill|Atilla Karaoğlan|tr}} ([[Turkish Football Federation|Türkiye]])
|result     = l
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GHA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Ghana
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GHA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Gambia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CIV}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Ivory Coast
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|CIV}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Gambia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Gambia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Somalia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
| Head Coach
|{{flagicon|NIR}} [[Johnny McKinstry]]
|-
| Assistant Coaches 
|{{flagicon|GAM}} Alagie Sarr<br>{{flagicon|GAM}} Abdou Jammeh
|-
| Goalkeeping Coach 
|{{flagicon|GAM}} Alhagie Marong
|-
| Fitness Coach
|{{flagicon|KEN}} Mikael Igendia
|-
| Match Analyst 
|{{flagicon|IRE}} Milad Samadi
|-
| Doctors
|{{flagicon|GAM}} Dr. Ousman Colley<br>{{flagicon|GAM}} Dr. Alhagie Jawara
|-
| Physiotherapists 
|{{flagicon|GAM}} Ebrima Jallow<br>{{flagicon|GAM}} Modou Jarra<br>{{flagicon|GAM}} Alassan Fofanah<br>{{flagicon|GAM}} Hassan Darboe
|-
| Team Coordinator 
|{{flagicon|GAM}} Ousman Drammeh
|-
| Technical Director 
|{{flagicon|GAM}} [[Sang Ndong]]
|}

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}

* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Ousmane Saho]] (1961)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Abdoulie Ngum]] and [[Moses Trinn]] (1970–1971)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Kebba Njie]] (1977)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Cherno Touray]] (1975–1978)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Mass Axi Gai]] (1978)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Kabba Jallow]] (1978–1983)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Saihou Sarr]] (1983)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Holger Obermann]] (1985)
* {{flagicon|SUI}} [[Hans Heiniger]] (1987–1992)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Sang Ndong]] (1992–1994)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Sang Ndong]] (1994–2003)
* {{flagicon|GER}} [[Antoine Hey]] (2006–2007)
* {{flagicon|ESP}} [[José Martínez (footballer, born 1942)|José Martínez]] (2007–2008)
* {{flagicon|BEL}} [[Paul Put]] (2008–2011)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Peter Bonu Johnson]] (2011–2012)
* {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Luciano Mancini]] (2012–2013)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Peter Bonu Johnson]] (2013–2015)
* {{flagicon|ESP}} {{flagicon|SUI}} [[Raoul Savoy]] (2015)
* {{flagicon|GAM}} [[Sang Ndong]] (2016–2018)
* {{flagicon|BEL}} [[Tom Saintfiet]] (2018–2024)
* {{flagicon|NIR}} [[Johnny McKinstry]] (2024–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition match|friendly]] matches against [[Iran national football team|Iran]] on 29 May 2026.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1573075601487754&set=a.514916530637005 |title=Gambia coach Jonathan McKinstry has announced a 25-man squad that will be playing in friendly matches during the June FIFA international window |website=Facebook |publisher=Gambia Football Federation |date=20 May 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 29 May 2026, after the match against [[Iran national football team|Iran]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Baboucarr Gaye]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|2|24}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[KF Shkëndija|Shkëndija]]|clubnat=MKD}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ebrima Jarju]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|03|16}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Paide Linnameeskond|Paide]]|clubnat=EST}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Sheikh Sibi]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|02|21}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Virtus Verona]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Omar Colley]] |age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1992|10|24}}|caps=65|goals=2|club=[[Al-Diriyah Club|Al-Diriyah]]|clubnat=KSA|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sainey Sanyang]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|04|18}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[PFC CSKA Sofia|CSKA Sofia]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Alagie Saine]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|1|20}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[AC Horsens|Horsens]]|clubnat=DEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Yaya Bojang]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|09|10|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Odense Boldklub|Odense]]|clubnat=DEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Tijan Sonha]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|07|31}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[PFC Beroe Stara Zagora|Beroe Stara Zagora]]|clubnat=BUL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abubacarr Sedi Kinteh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2006|11|30}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Tromsø IL|Tromsø]]|clubnat=NOR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Elvin Mendy]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2006|12|23}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[İstanbulspor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ebou Adams]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|01|15}}|caps=31|goals=2|club=[[Derby County F.C.|Derby County]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mahmudu Bajo]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|08|15}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Red Star Belgrade]]|clubnat=SRB}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abubakr Barry]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|7|2}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[FK Austria Wien|Austria Wien]]|clubnat=AUT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mouhamadou Drammeh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1999|5|15}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[FC Universitatea Cluj|Universitatea Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Jesper Ceesay]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|3|4}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[IFK Norrköping]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Karamba Gassama]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2005|1|2}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Gaziantep F.K.|Gaziantep]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ebrima Colley]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|2|1}}|caps=29|goals=2|club=[[BSC Young Boys|Young Boys]]|clubnat=SUI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Abdoulie Sanyang]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1999|05|08}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[HNK Hajduk Split|Hajduk Split]]|clubnat=CRO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yankuba Minteh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|07|22}}|caps=20|goals=7|club=[[Brighton & Hove Albion F.C.|Brighton & Hove Albion]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Alieu Fadera]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|11|03}}|caps=17|goals=1|club=[[US Sassuolo Calcio|Sassuolo]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Adama Sidibeh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|06|25}}|caps=10|goals=7|club=[[St Johnstone F.C.|St Johnstone]]|clubnat=SCO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Abdoulie Ceesay]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|1|5}}|caps=8|goals=3|club=[[FC St. Pauli]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Abdoulie Manneh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|9|29}}|caps=6|goals=3|club=[[Mjällby AIF|Mjällby]]|clubnat=SWE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Adama Bojang]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2004|5|28}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Stade de Reims|Reims]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Suleiman Camara]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|12|7}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Racing de Santander]]|clubnat=SPA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Salieu Drammeh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2003|3|28}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Lillestrøm SK|Lillestrøm]]|clubnat=NOR}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Gambia squad within the last twelve months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Jordi Bilali Danso]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2007|4|23}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Girona FC B|Girona B]]|clubnat=ESP|latest=v. {{fb|KUW}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sheriff Sinyan]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|7|19}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[CFR Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU|latest=v. {{fb|SEN}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Joseph Ceesay]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|6|3}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Empoli Calcio|Empoli]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|SEN}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Muhammed Sanneh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2000|02|19}}|caps=19|goals=0|club=[[FC Abdysh-Ata|Abdysh-Ata]]|clubnat=KGZ|latest=v. {{fb|KUW}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[James Gomez (footballer)|James Gomez]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|11|14}}|caps=26|goals=1|club=[[Odense Boldklub|Odense]]|clubnat=DEN|latest=v. {{fb|SEY}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Momodou Lion Njie]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|12|10}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[KFUM-Kameratene Oslo|KFUM Oslo]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|SEY}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Sheriff Sinyan]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1996|7|19}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[CFR Cluj]]|clubnat=ROU|latest=v. {{fb|BDI}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Alasana Manneh]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|04|08}}|caps=21|goals=1|club=[[Hibernian F.C.|Hibernian]]|clubnat=SCO|latest=v. {{fb|KUW}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Musa Barrow]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|11|14}}|caps=54|goals=14|club=[[Al Taawoun FC|Al Taawoun]]|clubnat=SAU|latest=v. {{fb|SEN}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Youssoupha Sanyang]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2005|8|31}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Östers IF|Öster]]|clubnat=SWE|latest=v. {{fb|SEN}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Musa Juwara]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|2001|12|26}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Pogoń Szczecin]]|clubnat=POL|latest=v. {{fb|KUW}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Gibril Sillah]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1998|9|7}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|KUW}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ali Sowe]]|age={{birth date and age|df=yes|1994|6|14}}|caps=21|goals=1|club=[[Çaykur Rizespor]]|clubnat=TUR|latest=v. {{fb|SEY}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>WD</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.<br/>
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br/>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br/>
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br/>
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end}}

== Records ==
{{updated|14 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Gambia |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/69/Gambia.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Gambia.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Omar Colley (cropped).jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Omar Colley]] is Gambia's most capped player with 62 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Omar Colley]]'''
|62
|1
|2012–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Musa Barrow]]'''
|52
|14
|2018–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Pa Modou Jagne]]
|43
|2
|2006–2022
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Assan Ceesay]]'''
|41
|13
|2013–present
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Bubacarr Sanneh]]'''
|40
|1
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=2|6
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ablie Jallow]]'''
|38
|8
|2015–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Ebrima Sohna]]
|38
|3
|2007–2022
|-
|8
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Sulayman Marreh]]'''
|35
|1
|2011–present
|-
|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Abdou Jammeh]]
|33
|2
|2006–2015
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Modou Jobe]]'''
|31
|0
|2007–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Musa Barrow (cropped).jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Musa Barrow]] is Gambia's top goalscorer with 14 goals.]]
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Musa Barrow]]'''
|14
|52
|{{#expr:14/52 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Assan Ceesay]]'''
|13
|41
|{{#expr:13/41 round 2}}
|2013–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Ablie Jallow]]'''
|8
|38
|{{#expr:8/38 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=2|4
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Adama Sidibeh]]'''
|7
|9
|{{#expr:7/9 round 2}}
|2024–present
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Yankuba Minteh]]'''
|7
|19
|{{#expr:7/19 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Momoudou Ceesay]]
|6
|16
|{{#expr:6/16 round 2}}
|2010–2015
|-
|rowspan=3|7
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Omar Samba]]
|5
|12
|{{#expr:5/12 round 2}}
|1994–2002
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Jatto Ceesay]]
|5
|17
|{{#expr:5/17 round 2}}
|1994–2007
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Mustapha Jarju]]
|5
|26
|{{#expr:5/26 round 2}}
|2006–2013
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Muhammed Badamosi]]'''
|4
|26
|{{#expr:4/26 round 2}}
|2018–present
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1950 FIFA World Cup|1950]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
|[[1954 FIFA World Cup|1954]] to [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]] to [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|3
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|rowspan=8 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|2
|3
|1
|6
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|6
|1
|1
|4
|4
|11
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 3
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 10
| 4
| 1
| 5
| 27
| 18
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|rowspan=2 colspan=6|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||–||{{Tooltip|0/14|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||–||–||–||–||–||–||36||10||7||19||48||57
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Gambia at the Africa Cup of Nations}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]] to {{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|colspan=8|''Part of United Kingdom''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]||colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]] to {{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Cup of Nations|1974]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]] to {{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Banned for withdrawing in 1996''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 African Cup of Nations|2002]] to {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|colspan=8|''Banned''
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]|| Quarter-finals|| 6th || 5 || 3 || 1 || 1 || 4 || 3
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]|| Group stage || 23rd || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 2 || 7
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]|| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total||Quarter-finals||2/35||8||3||1||4||6||10
|}

==Head-to-head record==
''As of match played 28 May 2026 after the match against [[Iran national football team|Iran]]''

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center"
|-
! Opponent
! {{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! {{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! {{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! {{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! {{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! {{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! {{Tooltip|GD|Goals difference}}
! {{Tooltip|W%|Win percentage}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Algeria}}
{{WDL|10|2|3|5|for=7|against=14|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Angola}}
{{WDL|5|2|1|2|for=6|against=5|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Benin}}
{{WDL|2|1|0|1|for=3|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Burkina Faso}}
{{WDL|5|1|3|1|for=4|against=5|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Burundi}}
{{WDL|2|1|0|1|for=4|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Cameroon}}
{{WDL|4|0|0|4|for=2|against=8|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Cape Verde}}
{{WDL|9|4|3|2|for=13|against=7|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Central African Republic}}
{{WDL|2|1|1|0|for=3|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Chad}}
{{WDL|2|1|1|0|for=3|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|China}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=2|against=4|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Comoros}}
{{WDL|2|0|1|1|for=2|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Congo}}
{{WDL|5|1|3|1|for=5|against=5|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|DR Congo}}
{{WDL|3|1|1|1|for=5|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Denmark}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=1|against=4|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Djibouti}}
{{WDL|2|0|2|0|for=2|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Equatorial Guinea}}
{{WDL|2|1|0|1|for=2|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Gabon}}
{{WDL|8|2|3|3|for=11|against=12|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Ghana}}
{{WDL|8|1|1|6|for=6|against=16|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Guinea}}
{{WDL|25|8|12|5|for=22|against=32|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Guinea-Bissau}}
{{WDL|12|4|2|6|for=12|against=13|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Iran}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=1|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Ivory Coast}}
{{WDL|8|2|0|6|for=5|against=21|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Kenya}}
{{WDL|2|1|1|0|for=6|against=4|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Kosovo}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Lesotho}}
{{WDL|2|1|0|1|for=6|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Liberia}}
{{WDL|10|3|3|4|for=11|against=14|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Libya}}
{{WDL|2|0|1|1|for=1|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Luxembourg}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=1|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Madagascar}}
{{WDL|2|1|1|0|for=2|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Mali}}
{{WDL|21|7|5|9|for=16|against=26|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Mauritania}}
{{WDL|21|8|8|5|for=26|against=20|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Mexico}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=5|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Morocco}}
{{WDL|8|1|1|6|for=2|against=14|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Namibia}}
{{WDL|4|1|1|2|for=5|against=5|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|New Zealand}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Niger}}
{{WDL|2|1|1|0|for=3|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Nigeria}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Saudi Arabia}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Senegal}}
{{WDL|27|0|11|16|for=11|against=40|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Seychelles}}
{{WDL|2|2|0|0|for=12|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Sierra Leone}}
{{WDL|20|5|6|9|for=15|against=23|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|South Africa}}
{{WDL|3|0|2|1|for=2|against=6|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|South Sudan}}
{{WDL|3|3|0|0|for=6|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Tanzania}}
{{WDL|2|1|0|1|for=3|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Togo}}
{{WDL|6|2|2|2|for=5|against=6|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#cfc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Tunisia}}
{{WDL|4|3|0|1|for=5|against=3|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Uganda}}
{{WDL|2|0|2|0|for=2|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#ffc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|UAE}}
{{WDL|1|0|1|0|for=1|against=1|diff=yes}}
|- bgcolor="#fcc"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|Zambia}}
{{WDL|1|0|0|1|for=0|against=2|diff=yes}}
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total
{{WDLtot|270|73|83|114|for=256|against=359|diff=yes}}
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Amilcar Cabral Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (3): 1980, 1985, 2001
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): 1993

== Notes ==
{{notelist}}

==References==
{{Reflist|2}}
{{Reflist|group=note}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* {{Official website|https://www.gambiaff.org/}} of the Gambian Football Federation
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/gam/ Gambia] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/GAM Gambia] at [[FIFA]]

{{Gambia national football team}}
{{Football in the Gambia}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of the Gambia}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Gambia National Football Team}}
[[Category:Gambia national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:National sports teams established in 1953]]
